en: Okay, what else we got? Hi Jimmy, I just wanted to say thank you.
The last six months you have helped me navigate PTSD
ko: 지미 지난 6개월간 호주에서 MDMA 치료와 함께 PTSD를 극복하는 데

en: along with MDMA therapy in Australia.
You're the cheapest therapy you can get.
You deserve every dollar. Cheers, man.
Dan, where's, where are you, Dan? Where's Dan?
Are you there, Dan? Oh, hi, Dan. How are you, man?
What, well, thanks. Thanks for the message. I really appreciate it.
How's the, how's the PTSD?
Bloody awful. Bloody awful. What, do you mind me asking, what do you get it from?
ko: 거기 있어? 안녕 댄 잘 지내?

en: Abuse. Oh man, I'm sorry to hear that, but you won't be the only person in the room.
The sad thing about that is there'll be other people here that have gone through something similar
and they haven't got to where you've got to yet, where you can send a message, where you can admit that it happened,
where you can talk about it. So you're on the road to recovery.
ko: 엄청 힘들어. 뭐 때문인지 물어봐도 돼? 학대.

en: Good luck, my friend. I'm sure I'm happy for you.
We do just have to confirm, when you say abuse,
ko: 이런 유감이야. 하지만 이 방에서 너만 그런 건 아닐 거야.

en: you were abused, right? It wasn't like, you're not going,
ko: 슬프지만 비슷한 경험을 한 다른 사람들이 있을 거야.

en: oh, I can't believe I got fucking caught. Bloody annoyed about it.
ko: 아직 네가 도달한 단계에 못 간 사람들. 메시지를 보내고 인정하고

en: Ah! I'm really glad you sent me the message.
I'm really glad you shared that, man. That's, I don't know, it's not me.
I've got nothing to, I've got dick jokes, but if it lightens the load in any way,
I'm fucking thrilled for you. Thank you so much.
That's fucking, it's a,
it's a fucking, if you think about it, that's a fucking brave thing.
